examples.title: Basic Backtracking Search for CSP
examples.description: Standard backtracking algorithm that assigns values to variables one at a time and backtracks on conflict
examples.code:
BACKTRACK(assignment, csp):
  IF assignment is complete:
    RETURN assignment
  
  var = SELECT-UNASSIGNED-VARIABLE(csp, assignment)
  
  FOR value in ORDER-DOMAIN-VALUES(var, assignment, csp):
    IF value is consistent with assignment:
      ADD {var = value} to assignment
      
      INFER = INFERENCE(csp, var, value)
      IF INFER ≠ failure:
        ADD inferences to assignment
        
        result = BACKTRACK(assignment, csp)
        IF result ≠ failure:
          RETURN result
      
      REMOVE {var = value} and inferences from assignment
  
  RETURN failure
examples.explanation: Standard backtracking: selects unassigned variable, tries each value in order, checks consistency, recurses. Returns solution or failure. Can be enhanced with heuristics (MRV, LCV) and inference (forward checking, AC-3).
examples.timeComplexity: O(d^n) worst case where d=domain size, n=variables

examples.title: AC-3 Algorithm for Arc Consistency
examples.description: Implementation of AC-3 algorithm to enforce arc consistency in CSP with binary constraints
examples.explanation: AC-3 maintains arc consistency by repeatedly checking each arc (Xi, Xj). For each value in Xi, it checks if there's a supporting value in Xj that satisfies the binary constraint. If not, the value is removed from Xi's domain. Returns False if any domain becomes empty.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(e + d) for queue and domains

examples.title: N-Queens CSP Solver with Backtracking
examples.description: C implementation of N-Queens using backtracking with constraint checking
examples.explanation: N-Queens solver: row by row, place queen in each row such that it doesn't conflict with previous queens. isSafe() checks column and both diagonals. Uses backtracking - when placement fails, remove queen and try next column. Finds first solution then returns.
examples.timeComplexity: O(N!) in worst case, much less with pruning

examples.title: Min-Conflicts Incomplete CSP Algorithm
examples.description: Local search algorithm for CSP that starts with complete assignment and minimizes conflicts
examples.explanation: Min-Conflicts is a local search (incomplete) algorithm: (1) Start with random complete assignment, (2) Find variable in conflict, (3) Change it to value with fewest conflicts, (4) Repeat. Fast for many problems like N-Queens but may not find solution or get stuck in local optima. Particularly effective when solution is dense in search space.
